headline: ACC Releases February 2026 Resin Production and Sales Statistics
summary_text: ACC Releases February 2026 Resin Production and Sales Statistics Rail Merger Threatens Economy Follow us # ACC Releases February 2026 Resin Production and Sales Statistics - Mar 27, 2026 - Read time: 1 minute - Press Release CONTACT US Jennifer Killinger ... WASHINGTON (March 27, 2026) – U.S. production of major plastic resins totaled 8.2 billion pounds during February 2026, a decrease of 7.8 percent compared to the prior month, and an increase of 2.7 percent compared to the same month in 2025, according to statistics released today by the American Chemistry Council (ACC). Year-to-date production was 17.1 billion pounds, a 5.0 percent increase as compared to the same period in 2025. Sales and captive (internal) use of major plastic resins totaled 8.1 billion pounds during February 2026, a 5.6 percent decrease compared to the prior month, and an increase of 0.7 percent from the same month one year earlier. Year-to-date sales and captive use were 16.6 billion pounds, a 1.9 percent increase as compared to the same period in 2025. PITTSBURGH, March 26, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Most Pittsburgh homeowners are told the same thing when their roof starts ... out of pocket, and tons of old roofing material hauled to a landfill. Liqua-Roof is changing that conversation. Launching in Pittsburgh this April, Liqua-Roof offers a different path: instead of tearing off an aging asphalt shingle ... layer, creating a finish that's stronger than new. The proprietary resin blend embeds the remaining existing granules, ... Liqua-Roof is also a sustainable choice. Roof replacement is one of the largest sources of construction waste in North America, with an estimated 11–13 million tons of non-biodegradable material sent to landfills every year. By preserving and resurfacing existing roofs instead of tearing them off, Liqua-Roof eliminates landfill waste, dumpsters, and tear-off debris, extending roof life while keeping tons of material out of landfills. Liqua-Roof's solutions are ideal for residential asphalt shingle roofs as well as select commercial applications, giving property owners a proven, science-driven path to extend roof life and reduce unnecessary replacement. "Eddie and Mike are bringing Liqua-Roof's reinforcement-beyond-renewal approach to a market that's ready for a smarter roofing solution," said founder Carmelo Marsala. "Pittsburgh homeowners now have access to proven resurfacing technology designed to extend roof life and reduce unnecessary replacement for a fraction of the time, cost and mess." About Liqua-Roof Founded by Carmelo Marsala, Liqua-Roof is a roof resurfacing company specializing in patented roof-resurfacing technology designed to restore and reinforce asphalt shingle roofs. Marsala was challenged to re-color a roof and created something that disrupted an industry. The Liqua-Roof system replaces lost granules, enhances impact resistance, extends roof life, and revitalizes appearance, without the cost or disruption of a full roof replacement. Performance is validated through industry-standard testing protocols, including ASTM impact resistance benchmarks. Backed by a 15-year warranty and powered by Granulock™ technology, Liqua-Roof delivers long-term protection designed for real-world conditions. HOUSTON, March 16, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Kraton Corporation, a leading global producer of specialty polymers and high-value bio-based chemicals derived from pine wood pulping co-products, announces a global price increase for all polymer products. The increase will range from $440/MT to $700/MT, or as contracts allow, depending upon polymer type and production location. These price increases reflect sharp and sustained increases in costs of raw materials, energy, and freight that Kraton cannot unilaterally absorb, attributable to market instability emanating from the current geopolitical situation. These price increases will be effective April 1, 2026. About Kraton Corporation Kraton Corporation is a leading global producer of specialty polymers and high-value performance products derived from renewable resources. Kraton's polymers are used in a wide range of applications, including adhesives, coatings, consumer and personal care products, sealants, and lubricants, and medical, packaging, automotive, paving and roofing products. As the largest global provider in the pine chemicals industry, the company's pine-based specialty products are sold into adhesive, road and construction and tire markets, and it produces and sells a broad range of performance chemicals into markets that include fuel additives, oilfield chemicals, coatings, metalworking fluids and lubricants, inks, and mining. Kraton offers its products to a diverse customer base in over 70 countries worldwide. ... headline: ACC Releases January 2026 Resin Production and Sales Statistics
summary_text: ACC Releases January 2026 Resin Production and Sales Statistics Rail Merger Threatens Economy Follow us # ACC Releases January 2026 Resin Production and Sales Statistics - Press Release - Read time: 1 minute - Mar 09, 2026 CONTACT US Jennifer Killinger - Email - 202-249-6619 CONTACT US WASHINGTON (March 9, 2026) – U.S. production of major plastic resins totaled 8.9 billion pounds during January 2026, an increase of 2.5 percent compared to the prior month, and an increase of 7.2 percent compared to the same month in 2025, according to statistics released today by the American Chemistry Council (ACC). Sales and captive (internal) use of major plastic resins totaled 8.5 billion pounds during January 2026, a 3.0 percent decrease compared to the prior month, and an increase of 3.1 percent from the same month one year earlier. A long-running question in global oil markets is resurfacing with the ouster of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro: ... of US sanctions? Maduro’s removal introduces a new layer of uncertainty that goes beyond politics and straight into physical oil flows, pricing risk and refinery economics. The immediate issue is not whether ... equilibrium, and China will soon feel the pain. Markets are not reacting to ideology. They are reacting to risk. With Maduro no longer in power, uncertainty now ... Shipping is the most obvious pressure point. Tanker owners and insurers have already grown cautious around sanctioned ... The result won’t be an overnight halt, but a gradual thinning of the trade with fewer willing participants, higher transaction costs, and deeper discounts required to compensate for risk. Simply put, if Venezuelan oil flows to China slow or stop, the impact will be felt most sharply by refiners optimized for cheap heavy crude. Replacing Venezuelan barrels is possible but not cost-neutral. Alternatives include heavy grades from Latin America, Canada and the Middle East (Saudi Arabia included). However, none come with the same discounts that Venezuelan oil has offered under sanctions. This means higher feedstock costs, tighter margins, and potential run cuts ... China’s state-owned refiners are better positioned to absorb the shock. Independent refiners, which have historically ... margins, not politics, determine long-term demand. If Venezuelan oil becomes more trouble than it’s worth, refiners will quietly move on. The Return of the US Gulf Coast? Ironically, Maduro’s removal may reopen a door that sanctions once slammed shut: US Gulf Coast refineries. Venezuelan crude is heavy and sour, precisely the type of oil many American refineries are configured to process. These facilities were built decades ago, long before the US shale boom, to run heavy imported crude from Venezuela, Mexico and the Middle East. US refineries can’t easily substitute light American crude for heavy grades without costly modifications. As a result, the US still imports significant volumes of heavy oil. If sanctions are eased or selectively restructured under a post-Maduro framework, Venezuelan crude could once again flow north to the US rather than east to Asia. That would realign trade routes, reduce China’s access to discounted barrels, and ease feedstock pressures on US refiners. Such a shift would not be immediate, nor guaranteed. But markets are already pricing the possibility. This moment is less about regime change than about the fragility of sanctions-dependent trade. Venezuela’s oil exports ... certain level of ambiguity. That ambiguity is now shrinking. For China, the lesson is straightforward: relying on ... post-Maduro transition can restore enough credibility to attract transparent buyers, insurers, and financiers. For global markets, the episode reinforces a broader trend. Sanctions don’t eliminate oil flows, but they distort them, pushing trade into darker channels that work until they don’t. When political conditions shift, those channels can collapse quickly. In the near term, expect volatility rather than clarity. Some Venezuelan cargoes will still move. Discounts may widen. headline: LyondellBasell seeks 35¢/lb hike for US PE through May
summary_text: seeking a total price increase of 35¢/lb for US polyethylene: Houston, 19 March (Argus) - LyondellBasell is seeking a total price increase of 35¢/lb for US polyethylene (PE) contracts over the next three months signaling that the company believes higher prices have some staying power. In an 18 March letter, LyondellBasell informed customers that in addition to the 10¢/lb increase it is planning for March contracts, it was revising its earlier 10¢/lb increase for April contracts higher to 15¢/lb and adding another 10¢/lb increase for May contracts. All other producers have announced a 10¢/lb increase for March, which one market participant described as "a slam dunk". headline: Economic downturn forces LyondellBasell to trim sustainability goals
summary_text: annual recycled/renewable polymer goal: LyondellBasell lowered its annual recycled/renewable polymer goal to 800,000 metric tons, from 2 million, and adjusted its 2030 Scope 1 and 2 emissions reduction target to 32 %, from 42 %. The company announced its intention to produce 2 million metric tons of recycled and renewable - based polymers per year earlier this decade, but current economic realities make that goal unrealistic, CEO Peter Vanacker said. Initial goals laid out in the last decade were increased soon after the height of the Covid-19 pandemic. The company also established net zero Scope 1 and 2 emissions targets for 2050 as part of a broader move to improve the sustainability of its products.

headline: CBN Slashes Ways & Means to N2.84trn as Cardoso Signals End of Fiscal Dominance - TV360 Nigeria
summary_text: CBN Slashes Ways & Means to N2.84trn as Cardoso Signals End of Fiscal Dominance - TV360 Nigeria File photo of CBN building. Foreign exchange inflows surge, reserves hit 13-year high, and 32 banks meet recapitalisation target amid sweeping reforms…. Nigeria’s central bank has sharply reduced its controversial Ways and Means financing, cutting the figure to N2.84 trillion from the N26.95 trillion inherited by the current administration one of the most dramatic fiscal adjustments ... Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Olayemi Cardoso, disclosed the development at the Monetary Policy Forum in Abuja, where he outlined the impact of a broad set of reforms aimed at restoring macroeconomic stability. ... According to him, curbing Ways and Means financing, a practice where the central bank lends directly to the government was a critical first step. The figure dropped to N3.51 trillion by December 2024 before declining further to N2.84 trillion by January 2026. The move, he explained, not only restored compliance with legal limits but also reinforced the independence of the central bank and signaled a clear break from past practices. FX reforms drive inflows and stability On the foreign exchange front, Cardoso said reforms introduced by the apex bank have begun to yield tangible results, particularly in boosting liquidity and restoring market confidence. Diaspora remittances have emerged as a standout performer, with monthly inflows through official channels tripling from about $200 million to $600 million. The CBN is targeting $1 billion in monthly remittances by the end of 2026, pointing ... He added that improved settlement systems and tighter regulatory controls have further strengthened FX inflows, while reforms in market operations have helped narrow the gap between official and parallel market rates to below 2 percent. Reserves hit strongest level in over a decade Nigeria’s external reserves have also rebounded strongly. Gross reserves rose from $38.34 billion in February 2025 to $50.12 billion in February 2026, a more than 30 percent increase and the highest level recorded in 13 years. Net external reserves saw an even more dramatic jump, climbing from $3.99 billion at the end of 2023 to $34.80 billion by the end of 2025. The balance of payments has similarly improved, recording a surplus of $4.59 billion in the third quarter of 2025, compared to a deficit earlier in the year. Banking sector strengthens Cardoso also highlighted progress in the banking sector, revealing that 32 banks have already met the CBN’s revised ... He said the development enhances the sector’s resilience and positions financial institutions to support long-term investments and economic growth, particularly as Nigeria pursues its ambition of becoming a $1 trillion economy. Reform momentum continues Despite the gains, the CBN governor cautioned that the reform journey is not yet complete. The next phase will focus on consolidating progress, including pushing inflation toward single-digit levels, maintaining exchange rate stability, ... He emphasized that achieving these goals will require continued coordination between monetary and fiscal authorities, as well as disciplined policy execution. ... Also speaking at the forum, Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of the Economy, Wale Edun, underscored the ... Edun added that as inflation eases and reforms take hold, there is a reasonable expectation that interest rates will gradually decline provided macroeconomic conditions continue to improve. Both officials agreed that sustaining Nigeria’s economic recovery will depend on strong collaboration across institutions, consistent policy direction, and a shared commitment to long-term stability. ### Related Articles ### Flooding Threatens 30 Nigerian States

headline: Central Illinois Faces Level 3 Severe Storm Risk With Baseball Size Hail, 75 MPH Winds and Possible Tornadoes Thursday Afternoon Through Night - Saluda Standard Sentinel
summary_text: Central Illinois Faces Level 3 Severe Storm Risk With Baseball Size Hail, 75 MPH Winds and Possible Tornadoes Thursday Afternoon Through Night - Saluda Standard Sentinel pl ILLINOIS — A significant severe weather upgrade has been issued for Thursday, placing central Illinois under a Level 3 risk zone, signaling a higher likelihood of dangerous storms capable of producing large hail, damaging winds, and even a few tornadoes. The updated outlook indicates a 30 to 44 percent probability of severe thunderstorms across the region, making this one of the more impactful setups so far this season. ## Central Illinois Now in Highest Risk Zone for Severe Storms The Level 3 risk area stretches across central Illinois, including cities such as Peoria, Bloomington-Normal, Champaign, Decatur, and surrounding communities. While not the dominant threat, tornado potential is still present within the Level 3 zone. ... The most active window for severe weather is expected between 3 PM and 11 PM Thursday. During this period, storms are ... ## Surrounding Areas Face Lower but Still Notable Risk Levels Areas outside the Level 3 zone, including parts of northern and southern Illinois, remain under lower risk categories. These regions could still see strong storms, but with less intensity compared to central areas. Level 2 zones carry a 15 to 29 percent chance of severe storms, with hail up to golf ball size and wind gusts near 65 mph possible. Level 1 areas face a lower threat but could still experience isolated storms with smaller hail and gusty winds. ## Residents Urged to Prepare Ahead of Severe Weather Threat With a wide range of severe weather hazards possible, residents across Illinois are encouraged to stay weather-aware and be ready to take action if warnings are issued. Having a safety plan in place and knowing where to seek shelter could make a critical difference if storms intensify quickly. By formalizing eligibility criteria, administrative oversight, and annual caps, the program seeks to balance fiscal stewardship with industry competitiveness. summary_text: Wisconsin launches film office and tax credits to boost local productions News Sports Community Station Weather Watch Now 23 Tue 24 Wed 41 # Wisconsin launches film office and tax credits to boost local productions --- by Elly Laliberte Mon, March 16th 2026 at 3:41 PM TOPICS: - Wisconsin Film office Tax credits Local productions Film industry University of Wisconsin Film incentives State budget MADISON, Wis. — Wisconsin's film office and tax credits are officially live, aiming to bring more productions to the state. The state Department of Tourism's new Film Wisconsin office will help recruit film productions and distribute $5 million in tax credits annually. Both initiatives were signed into law as part of the 2025-27 state budget. Aaron Greer, an independent filmmaker and University of Wisconsin-Madison professor who has championed film incentives ... "There's an old saying in Hollywood, it takes ten years to become an overnight success. There's a way it feels like ... folks," he said. State officials emphasize that filming brings immediate business to local communities through crew wages, equipment rentals, catering, and lodging. "The film tax credits are kind of a twofer. The state of Wisconsin is going to win when filmmakers bring their productions to Wisconsin," said Craig Trost, the communications director for Travel Wisconsin. "The second way we win is through what is known as 'set jetting:' those visitors that come because they're big fans of the show." To qualify for tax credits, film projects longer than 30 minutes must spend at least $100,000 in Wisconsin, while shorter projects need to spend $50,000. The credits are retroactive to Jan. 1, allowing productions that began filming earlier this year to apply. Film Wisconsin will review tax credit applications on a monthly basis, with the state now accepting submissions from interested productions. Greer believes the program could help stabilize Wisconsin's film industry. "It's really about creating an entire ecosystem," he said, adding that as a professor training the next generation of Wisconsin filmmakers. "Hopefully we'll show up more on screen and behind the camera." Wisconsin was previously one of four states that did not have a film office and one of 14 without a tax incentive. headline: StudioCanal Acquires Majority Stake in Lucky Red - Digital Cinema Report
summary_text: StudioCanal Acquires Majority Stake in Lucky Red - Digital Cinema Report -- StudioCanal, Europe’s leading film and television studio and Canal+ in house studio has today announced the acquisition of a majority stake in Lucky Red, one of Italy’s major Italian distribution and production companies. Established in 1987 in Rome by Andrea Occhipinti, Lucky Red initially operated as an independent art-house film distributor. It has since expanded its scope and now stands as a major player in Italy’s independent film distribution and production landscape. Over nearly four decades, Lucky Red has released more than 600 films, many of which have ... International Film Festival, the Academy Awards and the David di Donatello Awards. Its catalogue includes films from some of the world’s most acclaimed directors (Bong Joon-ho, Jafar Panahi, Joachim ... Payne, Todd Haynes, Stephen Frears, Asghar Farhadi). In 2024, Lucky Red was awarded ‘best international innovation for distribution’ at the third International Film Distribution Summit. Lucky Red and StudioCanal are building on a long-standing relationship that has bought audiences an array of cinematic hits from The Coen Brothers’ Inside Llewyn Davis to StudioCanal’s German production Heidi, and the acclaimed We Live in Time starring Florence Pugh and Andrew Garfield. With this acquisition, the partnership enters an exciting new phase, giving StudioCanal a unique opportunity to expand direct operations into a major European market, alongside its existing direct distribution and production presence across 11 European territories, Austria, Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Ireland, Luxembourg, Netherlands, Poland, Spain and the United Kingdom, as well as in Australia and New Zealand, with offices in the United States and China. For Lucky Red, the move unlocks the potential to board more ambitious projects, reinforcing its ability to attract both seasoned filmmakers and emerging talent, setting the stage for even greater creative and commercial success. Anna Marsh, CEO of StudioCanal and chief content officer of Canal+: “I am delighted that StudioCanal is deepening its partnership with the incredibly talented team at Lucky Red, one of Italy’s most vibrant film and television companies. The nonprofit behind the Milwaukee Film Festival – and operator of the Oriental Theatre since 2018 – has purchased the 111-year-old two-screen Downer Theatre, Milwaukee Film officials announced March 2. “Owning the Downer Theatre is a dream-come-true for Milwaukee Film,” Milwaukee Film Executive Director Susan Kerns said in a news release. “It affords our organization stronger financial footing and ensures our ability to provide the city we love with the film festivals and year-round programming it deserves. Shirley Sneve ICT FARGO — The Northern Plains Indigenous Film Festival debuts April 17 at the historic Fargo Theatre in Fargo, North Dakota, establishing a new home for Indigenous cinema in the Northern Plains and greater Midwest. Founded to create a platform for Indigenous storytelling through film, the festival centers contemporary Indigenous stories while bringing filmmakers, audiences, and creative professionals into the same room. The two-day event will feature Indigenous-made narrative, documentary, and short films, filmmaker conversations, and programming designed to help Indigenous creatives build sustainable careers. While Indigenous film festivals exist across the country, few are rooted in the Northern Plains. headline: Iowa revives film rebate program years after scandal, but independent filmmakers say it falls short
summary_text: Iowa revives film rebate program years after scandal, but independent filmmakers say it falls short CEDAR RAPIDS, Iowa (KCRG) - Iowa is launching a new film rebate program nearly two decades after its last incentive program ended in scandal. The two-year pilot program offers up to 30% back on qualifying expenses. To qualify, productions must come from Iowa studios, carry a budget of at least $1 million, film mostly in the state, and be made available to the public. The Iowa Economic Development Authority said the Renovo Media Group in Clear Lake, Iowa proposed the concept for the new rebate program. It is Iowa’s first major rebate program since the state abruptly ended its previous incentive program in 2009 after millions of dollars were misused. The state said the new program has more safeguards against fraud, including specifying allowable and non-allowable expenses. It also requires a production’s final budget be reviewed by a third-party before approval. Eric Dean Freese, director of the Cedar Rapids Independent Film Festival, said the program represents progress but ... “It’s baby steps,” Freese said. He said the new program is not competitive enough with other states. ... appreciates the work that’s being done by those smaller budget productions.” The new rebate program comes as the state recently put its Greenlight Grant for low-budget films on pause — a grant Freese said many filmmakers relied on. The state said it will be evaluating the Greenlight Grant program “to ensure it has met the goals to advance Iowa’s film and media industry.” Filmmaker Brittany Benedict directed ’ 5683,’ a romantic comedy filmed in Eastern Iowa with a local crew. ... opportunities in Iowa have allowed her to start her career. “It’s a smaller market, but that also allows for really amazing talent to shine through,” Allen said. “I think that ... The film is streaming on Amazon Prime. Freese said the film represents Iowa’s growing industry. He said filmmakers just need more resources. “We’re missing out on an opportunity to create jobs, to create work, and to create films that are a calling card to the type of talent and type of work that’s being done in Iowa,” Freese said. The application window for the pilot rebate program is open through March 16, 2026. Funding runs through 2027. In a statement to TV9, the Iowa Economic Development Authority said it hopes the program will support large-scale productions in the state. So far, it says it’s received four applications for the rebate program. #### Most Read #### Latest News

headline: Cindependent to steward Mariemont Theatre in new nonprofit era - Movers & Makers
summary_text: Cindependent to steward Mariemont Theatre in new nonprofit era - Movers & Makers The lights are still low, the seats familiar, the smell of popcorn unchanged. But behind the scenes at the Mariemont Theatre, a new chapter is beginning – one rooted in preservation, community care and a nonprofit belief that moviegoing ... For Cindependent, the Cincinnati-based nonprofit best known for the Cindependent Film Festival, stepping into stewardship of the historic Mariemont Theatre is less about acquisition than responsibility. The move expands the organization’s mission to support independent storytelling while protecting a neighborhood institution that has long ... “(Cindependent) has been uplifting and supporting independent filmmakers since 2017,” she said. “Now, we are excited to ... ## From festival to year-round film home Since its founding nearly a decade ago, Cindependent has grown from an annual festival into a year-round nonprofit offering film screenings, education initiatives and community-centered programs. West said the decision to take on a physical theater follows years of building an organization designed for long-term sustainability. “Cindependent has built sustainable film programs by balancing creative vision with real economics,” she said, noting that experience is especially important in an industry where theaters operate on narrow margins. “When thinking about movie theaters, what most people don’t realize is that about 50% of every ticket sold goes back to the film distributor,” West said. “That means theaters survive not just on attendance, but on smart, diversified revenue streams within robust communities.” Cindependent’s model includes ticket sales, concessions, special events, merchandise, memberships, grants and donations. “No single piece carries all the weight,” West said. That approach now guides the Mariemont Theatre’s transition into nonprofit stewardship. Located at 6906 Wooster Pike in the Village of Mariemont, the six-screen cinema will continue operating as a neighborhood theater, showing a mix of new releases, independent films, classics and family programming when Cindependent begins operating the venue this winter. For theatergoers, the emphasis is on continuity rather than change. West said there are no immediate plans to raise ... “The Mariemont Theatre has always been a place people care deeply about,” West said. “Stepping in as the new tenant ... Over the following months, Cindependent and community leaders worked together to listen, assess and plan. A “Save the ... Flavia Bastos, and is led by West. The Esquire Theatre Group, which currently operates the cinema, will transition out of the space as Cindependent assumes leadership of the venue. Cindependent is not purchasing the Mariemont Theatre but leasing the space and becoming its new tenant and operator. The land the cinema sits on has been owned by the Spinnenweber family for generations, who have also operated the ... West said the nonprofit structure allows the organization to think beyond short operating cycles. “It gives us the ability to reinvest in the building, expand programming and education, increase accessibility, and ... Next steps include assessing and repairing the space as needed, with progress tied to fundraising and community input. ... As part of that effort, Cindependent has launched Friends of the Mariemont Theatre, a membership program inviting the ... headline: Alamo Drafthouse to sponsor Heartland Film Festival for next 3 years
summary_text: , before the actual script -- Alamo Drafthouse to sponsor Heartland Film Festival for next 3 years Entertainment -- # Alamo Drafthouse to sponsor Heartland Film Festival for next 3 years Alamo Drafthouse (WISH Photo) INDIANAPOLIS (WISH) — Heartland Film on Thursday announced Alamo Drafthouse Cinema at The Square as the presenting sponsor of the Heartland Film Festival for the next three years. In addition to being the presenting sponsor, Alamo Drafthouse Cinema will host many of the screenings and events during the festival’s run in October 2026, October 2027, and October 2028. “I am incredibly grateful for the Alamo Drafthouse Cinema’s continued support of the Heartland Film Festival,” said Michael Ault, president of Heartland Film. “In 2025, their theaters, dining and hospitality far exceeded the expectations of our festival filmmakers, attendees, volunteers and staff. Over the next three years, I look forward to growing the Heartland Film Festival through their dedicated partnership.” Alamo Drafthouse Cinema opened on Nov. 18, 2024 at The Square. The location includes 13 screens, 4k projection, an arcade, a private screening room, locally-sourced food, and craft drinks. “Heartland Film Festival reminds us why movies matter with people sitting together, sharing a moment and leaving with something new. It values good films, good people and a place where filmmakers and audiences feel welcome,” said Fabio De La Cruz, CEO of Sojos Capital. The 35th Heartland Film Festival is set for Oct. 8 through Oct. 18, with screenings at Alamo Drafthouse Cinema and theaters across the Indianapolis area. The films and tickets will be available starting on Sept. 17 at HeartlandFilm.org. News at your fingertips! Stay informed with Indiana, Local, and National News straight to your inbox.

headline: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News
summary_text: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News Menu Menu - Reel Indie # The Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections - By Reel Chicago - January 15, 2026 Chicago’s creative community is about to gain a new kind of gathering space, one built around collaboration, sustainability, and shared stewardship. The Midwest Exchange, presented by Kinowerks and Midwest Film Festival, is launching as a values-driven initiative designed to keep professional creative resources in motion. The concept is simple but powerful: keeping equipment, materials, and knowledge from sitting idle or going to waste, and instead create a trusted environment where filmmakers and creative professionals can connect, exchange, and ... At its core, the Midwest Exchange emphasizes stewardship over ownership, sustainability in practice, professional care, and long-term investment in community. The goal is not just to host an event, but to strengthen the creative ecosystem itself. The partnership brings together two organizations with complementary strengths. Midwest Film Festival contributes more than two decades of nonprofit experience and credibility within the filmmaking community. Kinowerks provides purpose-built creative infrastructure, including screening rooms, studios, and thoughtfully designed event space tailored for production and collaboration. The Midwest Exchange is designed for working filmmakers, technicians, producers, directors, cinematographers, editors, post supervisors, theater and audio professionals, as well as emerging creatives committed to long-term careers. Participation is intentionally centered on alignment of values rather than visibility. The inaugural Midwest Exchange will take place February 21, 2026, from 12–4 p.m. at Kinowerks, 5645 North Ravenswood Avenue in Chicago. Organizers estimate the initiative will reach a substantial regional audience, supported by a combined community that includes tens of thousands of subscribers and followers, along with thousands of annual in-person attendees across both organizations’ programming. That built-in network gives the Exchange the potential to extend its impact well beyond a ... Rather than a traditional sponsorship model, involvement is framed as participation. Supporters are encouraged to engage on-site, contribute to authentic storytelling, and align with professional standards and sustainability in action. Support tiers are structured to reinforce access, infrastructure, and long-term creative growth across the Midwest. For more information or to get involved, contact Grover Derupe at grover.derupe@beesonventures.com. --- --- ALSO READ: 31 Candles reaches theaters with Hannah Welever producing --- ##### Share This: [Reel

headline: Reel Us Back In: Wisconsin Gives Film Tax Incentives

headline: CBN Slashes Ways & Means to N2.84trn as Cardoso Signals End of Fiscal Dominance - TV360 Nigeria
summary_text: CBN Slashes Ways & Means to N2.84trn as Cardoso Signals End of Fiscal Dominance - TV360 Nigeria File photo of CBN building. Foreign exchange inflows surge, reserves hit 13-year high, and 32 banks meet recapitalisation target amid sweeping reforms…. Nigeria’s central bank has sharply reduced its controversial Ways and Means financing, cutting the figure to N2.84 trillion from the N26.95 trillion inherited by the current administration one of the most dramatic fiscal adjustments ... Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Olayemi Cardoso, disclosed the development at the Monetary Policy Forum in Abuja, where he outlined the impact of a broad set of reforms aimed at restoring macroeconomic stability. Cardoso said his team met a challenging economic landscape on assumption of office but moved quickly to implement ... According to him, curbing Ways and Means financing, a practice where the central bank lends directly to the government was a critical first step. The figure dropped to N3.51 trillion by December 2024 before declining further to N2.84 trillion by January 2026. The move, he explained, not only restored compliance with legal limits but also reinforced the independence of the central bank and signaled a clear break from past practices. FX reforms drive inflows and stability On the foreign exchange front, Cardoso said reforms introduced by the apex bank have begun to yield tangible results, particularly in boosting liquidity and restoring market confidence. Diaspora remittances have emerged as a standout performer, with monthly inflows through official channels tripling from about $200 million to $600 million. The CBN is targeting $1 billion in monthly remittances by the end of 2026, pointing ... He added that improved settlement systems and tighter regulatory controls have further strengthened FX inflows, while reforms in market operations have helped narrow the gap between official and parallel market rates to below 2 percent. Reserves hit strongest level in over a decade Nigeria’s external reserves have also rebounded strongly. Gross reserves rose from $38.34 billion in February 2025 to $50.12 billion in February 2026, a more than 30 percent increase and the highest level recorded in 13 years. Net external reserves saw an even more dramatic jump, climbing from $3.99 billion at the end of 2023 to $34.80 billion by the end of 2025. The balance of payments has similarly improved, recording a surplus of $4.59 billion in the third quarter of 2025, compared to a deficit earlier in the year. Banking sector strengthens Cardoso also highlighted progress in the banking sector, revealing that 32 banks have already met the CBN’s revised ... He said the development enhances the sector’s resilience and positions financial institutions to support long-term investments and economic growth, particularly as Nigeria pursues its ambition of becoming a $1 trillion economy. Reform momentum continues Despite the gains, the CBN governor cautioned that the reform journey is not yet complete. The next phase will focus on consolidating progress, including pushing inflation toward single-digit levels, maintaining exchange rate stability, ... He emphasized that achieving these goals will require continued coordination between monetary and fiscal authorities, as well as disciplined policy execution. ... Also speaking at the forum, Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of the Economy, Wale Edun, underscored the ... Edun added that as inflation eases and reforms take hold, there is a reasonable expectation that interest rates will gradually decline provided macroeconomic conditions continue to improve. Both officials agreed that sustaining Nigeria’s economic recovery will depend on strong collaboration across institutions, consistent policy direction, and a shared commitment to long-term stability. ### Related Articles ### Flooding Threatens 30

headline: Gov. Evers celebrates film tax credit launch at Micon Cinema in Eau Claire
summary_text: Gov. Evers celebrates film tax credit launch at Micon Cinema in Eau Claire EAU CLAIRE, Wis. (WEAU) - Governor Evers came to Eau Claire to celebrate the launch of Wisconsin’s film tax credit program. The Governor visited Micon Cinema Thursday to tell us a little bit about what this means for the state. Wisconsin’s new film office and tax credit incentives was signed into law last year as part of the 2025-2027 biannual budget. Evers says he wants to encourage filmmakers to move their high-dollar productions out of Hollywood and into the Badger State. “I think it’s an economic thing, I think it’s an artist thing. But at the end of the day, I just think there’s lots of people in Wisconsin that care about this field, and we’re gonna be able to improve our economy, but also improve that system and make it stronger,” said Evers. The Governor says Wisconsin’s Door County has already had a hint of the impact movies can make on the local economy. Last year, Great American Family’s “A Wisconsin Christmas Pie” was filmed entirely on-site at real Door County locations. He says it brought the area big business during one of the slowest seasons of the year. #### Most Read #### Latest News

summary_text: Iowa revives film rebate program years after scandal, but independent filmmakers say it falls short CEDAR RAPIDS, Iowa (KCRG) - Iowa is launching a new film rebate program nearly two decades after its last incentive program ended in scandal. The two-year pilot program offers up to 30% back on qualifying expenses. To qualify, productions must come from Iowa studios, carry a budget of at least $1 million, film mostly in the state, and be made available to the public. The Iowa Economic Development Authority said the Renovo Media Group in Clear Lake, Iowa proposed the concept for the new rebate program. It is Iowa’s first major rebate program since the state abruptly ended its previous incentive program in 2009 after millions of dollars were misused. The state said the new program has more safeguards against fraud, including specifying allowable and non-allowable expenses. It also requires a production’s final budget be reviewed by a third-party before approval. summary_text: Cindependent to steward Mariemont Theatre in new nonprofit era - Movers & Makers The lights are still low, the seats familiar, the smell of popcorn unchanged. But behind the scenes at the Mariemont Theatre, a new chapter is beginning – one rooted in preservation, community care and a nonprofit belief that moviegoing ... For Cindependent, the Cincinnati-based nonprofit best known for the Cindependent Film Festival, stepping into stewardship of the historic Mariemont Theatre is less about acquisition than responsibility. The move expands the organization’s mission to support independent storytelling while protecting a neighborhood institution that has long ... “(Cindependent) has been uplifting and supporting independent filmmakers since 2017,” she said. “Now, we are excited to ... ## From festival to year-round film home Since its founding nearly a decade ago, Cindependent has grown from an annual festival into a year-round nonprofit offering film screenings, education initiatives and community-centered programs. West said the decision to take on a physical theater follows years of building an organization designed for long-term sustainability. “Cindependent has built sustainable film programs by balancing creative vision with real economics,” she said, noting that experience is especially important in an industry where theaters operate on narrow margins. “When thinking about movie theaters, what most people don’t realize is that about 50% of every ticket sold goes back to the film distributor,” West said. “That means theaters survive not just on attendance, but on smart, diversified revenue streams within robust communities.” Cindependent’s model includes ticket sales, concessions, special events, merchandise, memberships, grants and donations. “No single piece carries all the weight,” West said. That approach now guides the Mariemont Theatre’s transition into nonprofit stewardship. Located at 6906 Wooster Pike in the Village of Mariemont, the six-screen cinema will continue operating as a neighborhood theater, showing a mix of new releases, independent films, classics and family programming when Cindependent begins operating the venue this winter. For theatergoers, the emphasis is on continuity rather than change. Cindependent is not purchasing the Mariemont Theatre but leasing the space and becoming its new tenant and operator. headline: Alamo Drafthouse to sponsor Heartland Film Festival for next 3 years
summary_text: , before the actual script -- Alamo Drafthouse to sponsor Heartland Film Festival for next 3 years Entertainment -- # Alamo Drafthouse to sponsor Heartland Film Festival for next 3 years Alamo Drafthouse (WISH Photo) INDIANAPOLIS (WISH) — Heartland Film on Thursday announced Alamo Drafthouse Cinema at The Square as the presenting sponsor of the Heartland Film Festival for the next three years. In addition to being the presenting sponsor, Alamo Drafthouse Cinema will host many of the screenings and events during the festival’s run in October 2026, October 2027, and October 2028. “I am incredibly grateful for the Alamo Drafthouse Cinema’s continued support of the Heartland Film Festival,” said Michael Ault, president of Heartland Film. “In 2025, their theaters, dining and hospitality far exceeded the expectations of our festival filmmakers, attendees, volunteers and staff. Over the next three years, I look forward to growing the Heartland Film Festival through their dedicated partnership.” Alamo Drafthouse Cinema opened on Nov. 18, 2024 at The Square. The location includes 13 screens, 4k projection, an arcade, a private screening room, locally-sourced food, and craft drinks. “Heartland Film Festival reminds us why movies matter with people sitting together, sharing a moment and leaving with something new. It values good films, good people and a place where filmmakers and audiences feel welcome,” said Fabio De La Cruz, CEO of Sojos Capital. The 35th Heartland Film Festival is set for Oct. 8 through Oct. 18, with screenings at Alamo Drafthouse Cinema and theaters across the Indianapolis area. headline: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News
summary_text: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News Menu Menu - Reel Indie # The Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections - By Reel Chicago - January 15, 2026 Chicago’s creative community is about to gain a new kind of gathering space, one built around collaboration, sustainability, and shared stewardship. The Midwest Exchange, presented by Kinowerks and Midwest Film Festival, is launching as a values-driven initiative designed to keep professional creative resources in motion. The concept is simple but powerful: keeping equipment, materials, and knowledge from sitting idle or going to waste, and instead create a trusted environment where filmmakers and creative professionals can connect, exchange, and ... At its core, the Midwest Exchange emphasizes stewardship over ownership, sustainability in practice, professional care, and long-term investment in community. The goal is not just to host an event, but to strengthen the creative ecosystem itself. The partnership brings together two organizations with complementary strengths. Midwest Film Festival contributes more than two decades of nonprofit experience and credibility within the filmmaking community. Kinowerks provides purpose-built creative infrastructure, including screening rooms, studios, and thoughtfully designed event space tailored for production and collaboration. The Midwest Exchange is designed for working filmmakers, technicians, producers, directors, cinematographers, editors, post supervisors, theater and audio professionals, as well as emerging creatives committed to long-term careers. Participation is intentionally centered on alignment of values rather than visibility. The inaugural Midwest Exchange will take place February 21, 2026, from 12–4 p.m. at Kinowerks, 5645 North Ravenswood Avenue in Chicago. Organizers estimate the initiative will reach a substantial regional audience, supported by a combined community that includes tens of thousands of subscribers and followers, along with thousands of annual in-person attendees across both organizations’ programming. That built-in network gives the Exchange the potential to extend its impact well beyond a ... Rather than a traditional sponsorship model, involvement is framed as participation. Supporters are encouraged to engage on-site, contribute to authentic storytelling, and align with professional standards and sustainability in action. Support tiers are structured to reinforce access, infrastructure, and long-term creative growth across the Midwest. Author The foreign exchange reserves of India continue to act as a strong shield for the economy amid global turbulence. According to the Reserve Bank of India’s State of the Economy article published in its March 2026 bulletin, the country’s forex reserves stood at an impressive $709.75 billion as of March 13. The RBI said these reserves are adequate to cushion the country against external shocks such as geopolitical conflicts, trade tensions, or fluctuations in global commodity prices. The central bank stressed that while global uncertainties remain high, India’s overall ... Despite these global concerns, the article pointed out that India has significantly improved its ability to manage external shocks. The central bank noted that the economy’s resilience has strengthened over time, supported by strong growth, stable macroeconomic indicators, and efficient management of external sector buffers. Over the years, India has diversified its crude import sources and increased its domestic refining capacity, reducing its dependence on any single region. Several policy measures have been introduced to deal with disruptions in global fuel supply and make better use of local capacities when global supply chains are affected. The RBI’s commentary also mentioned the government’s plan to create an Economic Stabilisation Fund, which would act as ... The second advance estimates of GDP for 2025–26, based on the revised base year of 2022–23, show that real GDP grew by 7.6%, up from 7.1% in the previous year. Fuel consumption, logistics movement, and trade volumes all showed improvement. Notably, e-way bill generation ... reforms. In conclusion, while global events like the West Asia conflict and trade investigations by major economies pose risks to India’s external environment, the RBI’s assessment presents a reassuring picture. India’s large forex reserves, prudent macroeconomic management, and diversification efforts have built a strong economic foundation. National food safety regulator FSSAI has made certain revisions in its packaging guidelines for manufacturers labelling food packets. The new rules will come into force with effect from July 1, 2027. Key changes include guidelines on the ... The proposed changes are aimed at providing ensuring the dissemination of clear information to consumers while easing ... Here is a summary of the latest guidelines, as per an official notification dated March 24: ... - Full nutritional details, such as RDA percentage or the number of servings per pack, will no longer be mandatory on ... - Packages of less than 100 square centimetres in size will not be required to carry the regulator's logo - Large factory containers -- also known as bulk containers -- must clearly mention the words “NON-RETAIL CONTAINER” or “NOT FOR DIRECT SALE TO CONSUMER” to prevent their direct retail sale to end-consumers - These containers must display the regulator's logo, name, address and batch number, and storage instructions if ... - If not on label, details like ingredients, vegetarian/non-vegetarian status and net quantity must be provided in ... - All information must be traceable and accessible, even digitally, if needed ... ## What is the purpose of these FSSAI guidelines? They are aimed at: - Enhancing clarity and transparency for consumers - Simplifying compliance and labelling for businesses ... Here are answers to frequently asked questions (FAQs) on the subject: When will FSSAI's new rules apply? The updated guidelines will take effect from July 1, 2027. What will the food regulator's incoming directions change? FSSAI has revised packaging and labelling rules to improve clarity and simplify compliance. ... Are there any changes for small and bulk packs? Small packs under 100 sq cm don’t need the FSSAI logo. Bulk containers must clearly state “NON-RETAIL CONTAINER” and ... Rising incomes, rapid urbanisation and evolving consumer preferences are positioning packaging as a key proxy for consumption trends. Demand is being propelled by end-use sectors such as food and beverages, pharmaceuticals, personal care, agriculture, consumer durables and e-commerce. The expansion of organised retail and quick commerce platforms is further accelerating growth. Flexible plastic packaging continues to dominate the market, particularly due to strong demand from FMCG and packaged food companies. However, rigid plastics are expected to be the fastest-growing segment. At the same time, paper-based packaging is gaining traction amid increasing focus on sustainability and recyclability. Packaging is also evolving beyond its functional role, emerging as a critical tool for branding and enhancing customer experience, especially with the rise of premium products. headline: India's Packaging Market to Reach $92 Billion by FY30: Report
summary_text: India's Packaging Market to Reach $92 Billion by FY30: Report ## Packaging market set to hit $92 bn by FY30 on consumption boom: Report India's packaging industry is projected to grow to $92 billion by FY30, driven by consumption, e-commerce, and sustainability trends. > "India is currently the fastest-growing packaging market globally - Avendus Capital Report" New Delhi, March 24 India's consumption boom is set to significantly drive the growth of the domestic packaging industry, which is projected to reach $92 billion by FY30, according to a report released on Tuesday. The report by Avendus Capital has estimated that the Indian packaging market will grow at a CAGR of around 9 per cent over the next five years, outpacing the country's GDP growth by nearly 1.3 times. The sector is increasingly being seen as a direct proxy for consumption trends, supported by rising incomes, urbanisation, and changing consumer preferences, it said. Demand is being fuelled by key end-use segments such as food and beverages, pharmaceuticals, personal care, agriculture, durables, and e-commerce, along with the rapid expansion of organised retail and quick commerce platforms. The report noted that India is currently the fastest-growing packaging market globally, even as per capita consumption remains significantly lower than developed economies, indicating strong headroom for future growth. Among segments, rigid plastic packaging is expected to emerge as the fastest-growing category, while flexible plastic packaging continues to dominate the market share, driven by demand from FMCG and packaged food companies. At the same time, paper-based packaging is gaining traction amid a broader shift toward recyclable and sustainable materials. The report also pointed out that packaging is increasingly moving beyond its traditional functional role to become a key tool for branding and customer experience, especially as companies focus on premiumisation. headline: Perfectpac Limited to Invest ₹125 Crore in New Greater Noida Manufacturing Unit
summary_text: Perfectpac Limited to Invest ₹125 Crore in New Greater Noida Manufacturing Unit Connect with us Greater Noida, March 14, 2026: BSE-listed packaging firm Perfectpac Limited has announced a major expansion of its operations with the planned establishment of a second manufacturing facility. The company, a prominent manufacturer of corrugated cartons and containers, has received a Letter of Intent (LoI) from the Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority (YEIDA) dated March 13, 2026, for the allocation of a suitable land plot for the project, it said ... The expansion comes as the company’s existing plant at Ecotech-II, Greater Noida, reaches its full production capacity of 27,000 MT per annum. To meet rising market demand, the proposed second unit will feature a significantly higher capacity of 42,000 MT per annum. The project involves an estimated investment of ₹125 crore and is expected to generate employment for approximately 700 people through both direct and indirect roles. Perfectpac has established a high-profile client base, supplying packaging solutions to leading global and domestic brands including LG, Samsung, Voltas, Usha, Motorola, Ahuja Radios, BTW, Hamdard, Apollo Pipes, Puma, Havells, and Lenovo. The new facility is intended to bolster this service capability by integrating state-of-the-art technology and modern machinery to ensure high-quality output. In a strategic move to diversify its product portfolio, the company also plans to use the new facility to expand into the manufacturing of multicolor cartons. This addition will allow Perfectpac to cater to premium packaging requirements and strengthen its overall competitive position within the corrugated packaging industry. summary_text: Food makers, exporters see higher packaging costs - The HinduBusinessLine ##### SENSEX 75,001.75 -1,702.38 ##### NIFTY 23,264.85 -512.95 ##### CRUDEOIL 9,002.00 + 4.00 ##### GOLD 152,130.00 -895.00 ... ##### NIFTY 23,264.85 -512.95 ##### CRUDEOIL 9,002.00 + 4.00 ##### CRUDEOIL 9,002.00 + 4.00 ##### GOLD 152,130.00 -895.00 # Food makers, exporters see higher packaging costs ## Players say if crude oil voltality persists they will need to pass on costs to consumers Updated - March 10, 2026 at 08:02 PM. | New Delhi × ### Share ... https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/companies/food-makers-exporters-see-higher-packaging-costs/article70727160.eceCopy Some exporters have recommended that polymer granules should be allowed to be imported at zero duty temporarily to curb ... Packaged food makers and exporters have begun witnessing a 15-20 per cent increase in packaging costs due to the crude oil price volatility amid the ongoing West Asia conflict. Companies said that if the volatility persists, then higher costs will be passed on to consumers. At the same time, some exporters have also recommended that polymer granules ... Sanjay Gupta, Senior Vice-President, Packaging Development, DS Group, pointed out that volatility in crude oil prices, coupled with prevailing geopolitical uncertainties, is beginning to have a visible impact on the packaging ecosystem, particularly in flexible and rigid plastic packaging, which are directly linked to petrochemical derivatives. “In the last few days, the increase in key polymer prices has started cascading through the value chain, and overall packaging costs in certain categories have already increased by more than 20 per cent. Apart from the increase in list prices of resins, the market is also witnessing premium being charged over list prices in some cases as tightening availability ... industry,” he added. Gupta said DS Group is closely monitoring these developments and working with partners to ensure ... FMCG players, which typically have 1-2 months of stocks of packaging materials, said if the ongoing volatility persists, they will have to pass on costs to consumers by hiking prices. Mayank Shah, Vice-President, Parle Products, said: “Costs of packaging materials have gone up by 15-20 per cent. If the volatility prevails, it will be difficult to absorb these costs, and one will have to pass it on to consumers. We are closely monitoring the developments.” Meanwhile, exporters, too are beginning to see higher packaging cost pressures. BV Krishna Rao, President, The Rice Exporters Association (TREA), said: “The share of packaging cost to the overall product costs has moved to 5 per cent from 3 per cent, and exporters are bearing it since they cannot pass on to buyers for quantity already contracted. Polymer granules, used for packing material manufacturing, should be allowed import at zero duty for a short span of time to ease these pressures.” Published on March 10, 2026 READ MORE

headline: India's packaging industry to reach $92 billion by FY30: Avendus, ETInfra
summary_text: India's packaging market is projected to reach $92 billion by FY30, growing at 9% CAGR, outpacing GDP growth. Growth driven by food, pharmaceuticals, personal care, agriculture, durables, and e-commerce sectors. Rigid plastic packaging is the fastest-growing segment, with paper packaging gaining due to sustainability.

headline: Packaging Market Size & Trends 2026-2035
summary_text: Global packaging market forecast to grow from USD 1.32 trillion in 2026 to USD 1.75 trillion by 2035 at 3.16% CAGR. Asia-Pacific, led by India and China, shows rapid growth driven by e-commerce. Innovations include biodegradable materials and plant-based plastics, with companies like Veritiv and WestRock leading sustainability efforts.

headline: Regulatory Update: BIS Mandates New Standards for Corrugated Boxes Effective April 2026
summary_text: India's Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) has issued new norms for strength and recyclability of packaging boxes, impacting suppliers like TCPL Packaging and Uflex. Non-compliant firms face certification delays, raising short-term supply risks for procurement managers.

headline: JK Paper Reports Record Q4 Profits Driven by Packaging Box Demand
summary_text: JK Paper announced a 25% year-on-year profit increase in Q4 FY26, attributed to surging demand for sustainable packaging boxes from FMCG and pharma sectors. The company plans capex of INR 500 Cr for new box manufacturing lines, signaling low financial risk.

headline: Uflex Faces Debt Restructuring After Finance Cost Surge; Packaging Outlook Stable
summary_text: Uflex Ltd reported higher debt due to rising interest rates but assured stable box production. Corporate finance challenges noted, yet strong order book from exports reduces risk for procurement strategies focusing on flexible packaging suppliers.

headline: Packman Industries Acquires Majority Stake in Shillim Packaging for Expansion
summary_text: Packman Industries, a leading corrugated box manufacturer in India, has acquired a 60% stake in Shillim Packaging to boost production capacity amid rising demand from e-commerce. This move strengthens their supply chain and positions them for better pricing negotiations in B2B segments.

headline: Mondi India Expands Rigid Box Capacity with Local JV Partnership
summary_text: Mondi Group formed a joint venture with an Indian firm to double rigid box output in Gujarat, focusing on premium pharma packaging. This partnership enhances regional supply reliability and opens negotiation leverage on sustainability premiums.

headline: Oji India Partners with UPM for Eco-Friendly Folding Box Innovations
summary_text: Oji India Packaging has entered a strategic partnership with Finland's UPM to introduce recyclable folding cartons using advanced barrier coatings. This innovation targets food and beverage clients, enhancing supplier appeal amid India's plastic ban regulations.

headline: TCPL Packaging Launches AI-Optimized Custom Box Line Amid Supply Chain Woes
summary_text: TCPL Packaging unveiled an AI-driven platform for rapid prototyping of custom rigid boxes, reducing lead times by 40%. This product innovation helps mitigate supplier risks from raw material shortages, beneficial for negotiations with automotive and retail buyers.

headline: EPL, Indovida to merge in $2 billion packaging deal
summary_text: merge: MUMBAI: EPL, a global leader in laminated plastic tube packaging backed by Blackstone, on Sunday said that it will merge with Indovida India, a rigid polyethylene terephthalate (PET) packaging platform owned by Indorama Ventures.The transaction will create a diversified, multi-format packaging platform with a combined valuation of about $ 2 billion.

headline: India's packaging industry to reach $92 billion by FY30: Avendus
summary_text: grow at 9 % compound annual growth rate (CAGR) over the next five years, reaching US $ 92 billion by financial year 2030: New Delhi: Fuelled by consumption - led growth, higher investor interest and deal activity, India's packaging industry is expected to grow at 9 % compound annual growth rate (CAGR) over the next five years, reaching US $ 92 billion by financial year 2030, a report by Avendus Capital noted.The report flagged India as the world's fastest growing packaging market, projected to outpace GDP growth by 1.3 times, driven by rising demand from end-use segments such as food and beverages, pharmaceuticals, personal care, agriculture, durables and e-commerce, along with growing penetration of organised retail and quick commerce.Also Read: Iran war unsettles India's packaged water makers as bottles, caps get priceyAccording to the report, globally, packaging is already a US $ 1 trillion industry.

Author The foreign exchange reserves of India continue to act as a strong shield for the economy amid global turbulence. According to the Reserve Bank of India’s State of the Economy article published in its March 2026 bulletin, the country’s forex reserves stood at an impressive $709.75 billion as of March 13. The RBI said these reserves are adequate to cushion the country against external shocks such as geopolitical conflicts, trade tensions, or fluctuations in global commodity prices. Despite these global concerns, the article pointed out that India has significantly improved its ability to manage external shocks. The central bank noted that the economy’s resilience has strengthened over time, supported by strong growth, stable macroeconomic indicators, and efficient management of external sector buffers. Over the years, India has diversified its crude import sources and increased its domestic refining capacity, reducing its dependence on any single region. Several policy measures have been introduced to deal with disruptions in global fuel supply and make better use of local capacities when global supply chains are affected. The RBI’s commentary also mentioned the government’s plan to create an Economic Stabilisation Fund, which would act as ... The second advance estimates of GDP for 2025–26, based on the revised base year of 2022–23, show that real GDP grew by 7.6%, up from 7.1% in the previous year. The RBI attributed this robust growth to healthy domestic demand, steady consumption, and ongoing investment in ... merchandise exports faced pressure from weaker demand and higher tariffs in the U.S. The article also reported that high-frequency indicators suggested strong momentum in economic activity during February 2026. Fuel consumption, logistics movement, and trade volumes all showed improvement. Notably, e-way bill generation ... reforms. In conclusion, while global events like the West Asia conflict and trade investigations by major economies pose risks to India’s external environment, the RBI’s assessment presents a reassuring picture. India’s large forex reserves, prudent macroeconomic management, and diversification efforts have built a strong economic foundation. headline: India's Packaging Market to Reach $92 Billion by FY30: Report
summary_text: India's Packaging Market to Reach $92 Billion by FY30: Report ## Packaging market set to hit $92 bn by FY30 on consumption boom: Report India's packaging industry is projected to grow to $92 billion by FY30, driven by consumption, e-commerce, and sustainability trends. > "India is currently the fastest-growing packaging market globally - Avendus Capital Report" New Delhi, March 24 India's consumption boom is set to significantly drive the growth of the domestic packaging industry, which is projected to reach $92 billion by FY30, according to a report released on Tuesday. The report by Avendus Capital has estimated that the Indian packaging market will grow at a CAGR of around 9 per cent over the next five years, outpacing the country's GDP growth by nearly 1.3 times. The sector is increasingly being seen as a direct proxy for consumption trends, supported by rising incomes, urbanisation, and changing consumer preferences, it said. Demand is being fuelled by key end-use segments such as food and beverages, pharmaceuticals, personal care, agriculture, durables, and e-commerce, along with the rapid expansion of organised retail and quick commerce platforms. The report noted that India is currently the fastest-growing packaging market globally, even as per capita consumption remains significantly lower than developed economies, indicating strong headroom for future growth. Among segments, rigid plastic packaging is expected to emerge as the fastest-growing category, while flexible plastic packaging continues to dominate the market share, driven by demand from FMCG and packaged food companies. At the same time, paper-based packaging is gaining traction amid a broader shift toward recyclable and sustainable materials. The report also pointed out that packaging is increasingly moving beyond its traditional functional role to become a key tool for branding and customer experience, especially as companies focus on premiumisation. The report further stated that investor interest in the sector remains strong, with steady deal activity and growing participation from private equity firms. Going forward, mergers and acquisitions are expected to play a crucial role in driving scale, enhancing capabilities, and tapping into high-growth segments, according to an analysis by Avendus Capital. Moreover, according to another, India's packaged food and beverages (F&B) market is set to enter a new phase of growth, projected to expand to over $150 billion by 2030 from around $100 billion currently, driven largely by the rapid rise of quick commerce. - IANS

headline: Perfectpac Limited to Invest ₹125 Crore in New Greater Noida Manufacturing Unit
summary_text: Perfectpac Limited to Invest ₹125 Crore in New Greater Noida Manufacturing Unit Connect with us Greater Noida, March 14, 2026: BSE-listed packaging firm Perfectpac Limited has announced a major expansion of its operations with the planned establishment of a second manufacturing facility. The company, a prominent manufacturer of corrugated cartons and containers, has received a Letter of Intent (LoI) from the Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority (YEIDA) dated March 13, 2026, for the allocation of a suitable land plot for the project, it said ... The expansion comes as the company’s existing plant at Ecotech-II, Greater Noida, reaches its full production capacity of 27,000 MT per annum. To meet rising market demand, the proposed second unit will feature a significantly higher capacity of 42,000 MT per annum. The project involves an estimated investment of ₹125 crore and is expected to generate employment for approximately 700 people through both direct and indirect roles. Perfectpac has established a high-profile client base, supplying packaging solutions to leading global and domestic brands including LG, Samsung, Voltas, Usha, Motorola, Ahuja Radios, BTW, Hamdard, Apollo Pipes, Puma, Havells, and Lenovo. The new facility is intended to bolster this service capability by integrating state-of-the-art technology and modern machinery to ensure high-quality output. In a strategic move to diversify its product portfolio, the company also plans to use the new facility to expand into the manufacturing of multicolor cartons. This addition will allow Perfectpac to cater to premium packaging requirements and strengthen its overall competitive position within the corrugated packaging industry. Packaged food makers and exporters have begun witnessing a 15-20 per cent increase in packaging costs due to the crude oil price volatility amid the ongoing West Asia conflict. Companies said that if the volatility persists, then higher costs will be passed on to consumers. At the same time, some exporters have also recommended that polymer granules ... Sanjay Gupta, Senior Vice-President, Packaging Development, DS Group, pointed out that volatility in crude oil prices, coupled with prevailing geopolitical uncertainties, is beginning to have a visible impact on the packaging ecosystem, particularly in flexible and rigid plastic packaging, which are directly linked to petrochemical derivatives. “In the last few days, the increase in key polymer prices has started cascading through the value chain, and overall packaging costs in certain categories have already increased by more than 20 per cent. Apart from the increase in list prices of resins, the market is also witnessing premium being charged over list prices in some cases as tightening availability ... industry,” he added. Gupta said DS Group is closely monitoring these developments and working with partners to ensure ... FMCG players, which typically have 1-2 months of stocks of packaging materials, said if the ongoing volatility persists, they will have to pass on costs to consumers by hiking prices. Mayank Shah, Vice-President, Parle Products, said: “Costs of packaging materials have gone up by 15-20 per cent. If the volatility prevails, it will be difficult to absorb these costs, and one will have to pass it on to consumers. We are closely monitoring the developments.” Meanwhile, exporters, too are beginning to see higher packaging cost pressures. BV Krishna Rao, President, The Rice Exporters Association (TREA), said: “The share of packaging cost to the overall product costs has moved to 5 per cent from 3 per cent, and exporters are bearing it since they cannot pass on to buyers for quantity already contracted. Over the years, India has diversified its crude import sources and increased its domestic refining capacity, reducing its dependence on any single region. Fuel consumption, logistics movement, and trade volumes all showed improvement. Notably, e-way bill generation ... reforms. The sector is expected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of around 9 per cent over the next five years, outpacing the country’s GDP growth by nearly 1.3 times. Rising incomes, rapid urbanisation and evolving consumer preferences are positioning packaging as a key proxy for consumption trends. Demand is being propelled by end-use sectors such as food and beverages, pharmaceuticals, personal care, agriculture, consumer durables and e-commerce. The expansion of organised retail and quick commerce platforms is further accelerating growth. Flexible plastic packaging continues to dominate the market, particularly due to strong demand from FMCG and packaged food companies. However, rigid plastics are expected to be the fastest-growing segment. At the same time, paper-based packaging is gaining traction amid increasing focus on sustainability and recyclability. Packaging is also evolving beyond its functional role, emerging as a critical tool for branding and enhancing customer experience, especially with the rise of premium products. Investor interest remains strong, with steady deal activity and increasing participation from private equity players. headline: India's Packaging Market to Reach $92 Billion by FY30: Report
summary_text: India's Packaging Market to Reach $92 Billion by FY30: Report ## Packaging market set to hit $92 bn by FY30 on consumption boom: Report India's packaging industry is projected to grow to $92 billion by FY30, driven by consumption, e-commerce, and sustainability trends. > "India is currently the fastest-growing packaging market globally - Avendus Capital Report" New Delhi, March 24 India's consumption boom is set to significantly drive the growth of the domestic packaging industry, which is projected to reach $92 billion by FY30, according to a report released on Tuesday. The report by Avendus Capital has estimated that the Indian packaging market will grow at a CAGR of around 9 per cent over the next five years, outpacing the country's GDP growth by nearly 1.3 times. The sector is increasingly being seen as a direct proxy for consumption trends, supported by rising incomes, urbanisation, and changing consumer preferences, it said. Demand is being fuelled by key end-use segments such as food and beverages, pharmaceuticals, personal care, agriculture, durables, and e-commerce, along with the rapid expansion of organised retail and quick commerce platforms. The report noted that India is currently the fastest-growing packaging market globally, even as per capita consumption remains significantly lower than developed economies, indicating strong headroom for future growth. Among segments, rigid plastic packaging is expected to emerge as the fastest-growing category, while flexible plastic packaging continues to dominate the market share, driven by demand from FMCG and packaged food companies. At the same time, paper-based packaging is gaining traction amid a broader shift toward recyclable and sustainable materials. The report also pointed out that packaging is increasingly moving beyond its traditional functional role to become a key tool for branding and customer experience, especially as companies focus on premiumisation. The report further stated that investor interest in the sector remains strong, with steady deal activity and growing participation from private equity firms. Going forward, mergers and acquisitions are expected to play a crucial role in driving scale, enhancing capabilities, and tapping into high-growth segments, according to an analysis by Avendus Capital. Moreover, according to another, India's packaged food and beverages (F&B) market is set to enter a new phase of growth, projected to expand to over $150 billion by 2030 from around $100 billion currently, driven largely by the rapid rise ... - IANS

headline: Perfectpac Limited to Invest ₹125 Crore in New Greater Noida Manufacturing Unit
summary_text: Perfectpac Limited to Invest ₹125 Crore in New Greater Noida Manufacturing Unit Connect with us Greater Noida, March 14, 2026: BSE-listed packaging firm Perfectpac Limited has announced a major expansion of its operations with the planned establishment of a second manufacturing facility. The company, a prominent manufacturer of corrugated cartons and containers, has received a Letter of Intent (LoI) from the Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority (YEIDA) dated March 13, 2026, for the allocation of a suitable land plot for the project, it said ... The expansion comes as the company’s existing plant at Ecotech-II, Greater Noida, reaches its full production capacity of 27,000 MT per annum. To meet rising market demand, the proposed second unit will feature a significantly higher capacity of 42,000 MT per annum. The project involves an estimated investment of ₹125 crore and is expected to generate employment for approximately 700 people through both direct and indirect roles. Perfectpac has established a high-profile client base, supplying packaging solutions to leading global and domestic brands including LG, Samsung, Voltas, Usha, Motorola, Ahuja Radios, BTW, Hamdard, Apollo Pipes, Puma, Havells, and Lenovo. The new facility is intended to bolster this service capability by integrating state-of-the-art technology and modern machinery to ensure high-quality output. In a strategic move to diversify its product portfolio, the company also plans to use the new facility to expand into the manufacturing of multicolor cartons. This addition will allow Perfectpac to cater to premium packaging requirements and strengthen its overall competitive position within the corrugated packaging industry. Packaged food makers and exporters have begun witnessing a 15-20 per cent increase in packaging costs due to the crude oil price volatility amid the ongoing West Asia conflict. Companies said that if the volatility persists, then higher costs will be passed on to consumers. At the same time, some exporters have also recommended that polymer granules ... Sanjay Gupta, Senior Vice-President, Packaging Development, DS Group, pointed out that volatility in crude oil prices, coupled with prevailing geopolitical uncertainties, is beginning to have a visible impact on the packaging ecosystem, particularly in flexible and rigid plastic packaging, which are directly linked to petrochemical derivatives. “In the last few days, the increase in key polymer prices has started cascading through the value chain, and overall packaging costs in certain categories have already increased by more than 20 per cent. Apart from the increase in list prices of resins, the market is also witnessing premium being charged over list prices in some cases as tightening availability ... industry,” he added. Gupta said DS Group is closely monitoring these developments and working with partners to ensure ... FMCG players, which typically have 1-2 months of stocks of packaging materials, said if the ongoing volatility persists, they will have to pass on costs to consumers by hiking prices. Mayank Shah, Vice-President, Parle Products, said: “Costs of packaging materials have gone up by 15-20 per cent. If the volatility prevails, it will be difficult to absorb these costs, and one will have to pass it on to consumers. We are closely monitoring the developments.” Meanwhile, exporters, too are beginning to see higher packaging cost pressures. BV Krishna Rao, President, The Rice Exporters Association (TREA), said: “The share of packaging cost to the overall product costs has moved to 5 per cent from 3 per cent, and exporters are bearing it since they cannot pass on to buyers for quantity already contracted.
